description: Deliver full RFC 5280/6960/6125/9618 compliance without sacrificing DX, tree-shaking, or edge/web compatibility.

tasks.title: Finish compliance matrix and docs audit
tasks.description:
Finish the implemented/partial/not-yet compliance matrix and audit all user-facing claims. Done when:
- README and PKIX scope classify RFC areas honestly
- every checked checklist item points to shipped behavior and tests
- no doc implies full 5280/6960/6125 compliance early

tasks.title: Investigate tsdown multi-entry exports
tasks.description:
Investigate how tsdown derives exports and multi-entry ESM output for this package. Done when:
- recommended tsdown config pattern is documented
- generated-exports behavior is confirmed
- package.json exports are confirmed to be generated, not hand-edited

tasks.title: Research RFC 5280 name comparison
tasks.description:
Research RFC 5280 section 7 comparison rules for directoryName and related name forms. Done when:
- required normalization rules are documented
- unsafe shortcuts are identified
- target helpers and fixtures are named

tasks.title: Design RFC 9618 policy engine
tasks.description:
Design the RFC 9618 policy state model before coding. Done when:
- state structures and transitions are documented
- explicitPolicy, anyPolicy, and policyMappings handling are mapped
- the plan does not depend on the old RFC 5280 policy tree

tasks.title: Map full CRL applicability semantics
tasks.description:
Map distributionPoint, issuingDistributionPoint, indirect CRL, and delta CRL semantics before implementation. Done when:
- clean-break CRL data model is documented
- applicability rules are listed
- required fixtures are enumerated

tasks.title: Define public validation boundaries
tasks.description:
Define stable public boundaries for path validation, identity matching, revocation, and policy inputs/results. Done when:
- shared boundary types are specified
- raw path validation excludes service identity inputs
- module ownership is explicit for verify, identity, and revocation

tasks.title: Scaffold identity module
tasks.description:
Create the `src/identity.ts` public boundary and export surface. Done when:
- module skeleton and public types exist
- root barrel and future subpath wiring are in place
- no behavior change is required yet

tasks.title: Scaffold revocation module
tasks.description:
Create the `src/revocation.ts` public boundary for high-level revocation orchestration. Done when:
- module skeleton and public result types exist
- ownership between CRL, OCSP, and revocation is explicit
- no network behavior is introduced

tasks.title: Add domain subpath entrypoints
tasks.description:
Add stable package entrypoints for verify, identity, revocation, parse, keys, CRL, and OCSP domains. Done when:
- tsdown entry map covers each public domain
- generated exports expose stable subpaths
- no manual package.json export editing is needed

tasks.title: Preserve module boundaries in build output
tasks.description:
Switch package build output from a single bundled entry to preserved ESM modules. Done when:
- build emits unbundled ESM modules
- root barrel still works
- subpath imports resolve from generated output

tasks.title: Set sideEffects metadata
tasks.description:
Enable and verify side-effect-free package metadata. Done when:
- `sideEffects: false` is added if valid
- build/tests confirm no behavior loss
- docs state the intended tree-shaking contract

tasks.title: Add policy-validation input types
tasks.description:
Add public policy-validation input types to verify APIs. Done when:
- initialPolicySet, requireExplicitPolicy, inhibitPolicyMapping, and inhibitAnyPolicy are typed
- exports include the new input shapes
- existing call sites still compile

tasks.title: Add initial name-constraint inputs
tasks.description:
Add initial permitted/excluded subtree input types for path validation. Done when:
- public inputs can carry initial subtrees
- exports include the new shapes
- default behavior remains explicit

tasks.title: Plumb RFC 5280 input state
tasks.description:
Thread the new policy and name-constraint inputs through candidate-path validation state. Done when:
- validation state carries all RFC 5280 section 6.1.1 inputs this spec requires
- defaults are explicit in code and docs
- setup/enforcement failures are typed

tasks.title: Split host identity from raw path validation
tasks.description:
Remove hostname/IP/CN-fallback inputs from raw path validation and move them to identity-oriented APIs. Done when:
- `validateCandidatePath` no longer accepts host identity fields
- path-only validation has direct tests
- migration path for profile wrappers is clear

tasks.title: Rewire TLS profile helpers
tasks.description:
Rewire TLS/client/server/profile helpers to compose path validation with the identity boundary. Done when:
- TLS helpers call the new identity layer
- purpose and identity failures remain typed
- caller ergonomics stay intact

tasks.title: Define GeneralName support matrix
tasks.description:
Define supported GeneralName forms and fail-closed behavior for name constraints. Done when:
- support matrix is explicit
- unsupported critical forms have deterministic handling
- parser vs validator responsibilities are documented

tasks.title: Fail closed on unsupported name constraints
tasks.description:
Stop silently bypassing unsupported name-constraint forms. Done when:
- critical under-enforced cases fail predictably
- failure codes are typed
- regression tests cover skipped-form cases

tasks.title: Implement RFC 5280 directoryName comparison
tasks.description:
Implement RFC 5280 section 7 directoryName comparison semantics. Done when:
- comparison no longer relies on raw DER prefix equality
- normalization helpers encode the required rules
- positive and negative DN fixtures pass

tasks.title: Apply initial subtree inputs
tasks.description:
Enforce initial permitted/excluded subtree inputs during path validation. Done when:
- initial subtrees seed accumulated constraints
- self-issued handling stays correct
- mixed chain/path tests pass

tasks.title: Add name-constraint fixtures
tasks.description:
Add focused fixtures for name constraints and name comparison. Done when:
- email, DNS, URI, IP, and directoryName cases exist
- positive and negative paths are covered
- tests document intended semantics

tasks.title: Add policy OIDs and types
tasks.description:
Add RFC policy OIDs and structured types for policy extensions. Done when:
- all four policy-related extensions have stable types
- OIDs live in the registry
- parse/build code can depend on the new shapes

tasks.title: Build policy-related extensions
tasks.description:
Implement builders and encoders for certificatePolicies, policyMappings, policyConstraints, and inhibitAnyPolicy. Done when:
- create flows can encode each supported policy extension
- invalid combinations and anyPolicy misuse are rejected
- builder tests cover edge cases

tasks.title: Parse policy-related extensions
tasks.description:
Implement parsers and decoders for policy-related extensions. Done when:
- parsed certificates expose structured policy data
- malformed encodings fail predictably
- raw extension preservation remains intact

tasks.title: Add policy extension round-trip tests
tasks.description:
Add round-trip coverage for policy extension parsing/building. Done when:
- each policy extension has positive round-trip tests
- malformed fixtures exist
- tests prove no required data loss

tasks.title: Implement RFC 9618 policy state model
tasks.description:
Implement the core RFC 9618 policy state machine and set model. Done when:
- internal state transitions are coded
- the algorithm is clearly RFC 9618-based
- performance does not depend on tree expansion

tasks.title: Enforce policy constraints
tasks.description:
Apply explicit-policy, policyConstraints, and inhibitAnyPolicy rules during validation. Done when:
- valid chains still pass
- invalid chains fail with typed policy errors
- edge-case fixtures cover each rule

tasks.title: Enforce policy mappings
tasks.description:
Apply policyMappings semantics and anyPolicy restrictions. Done when:
- direct and invalid mappings are enforced
- self-issued and multi-hop cases are covered
- failure details identify the policy cause

tasks.title: Expose policy validation outcomes
tasks.description:
Expose final policy outcomes through public validation APIs. Done when:
- callers can inspect constrained policy results or typed failures
- docs/types explain the result shape
- wrapper APIs preserve ergonomic defaults

tasks.title: Add RFC 9618 policy fixtures
tasks.description:
Add RFC 9618 policy validation fixtures and regressions. Done when:
- valid and invalid policy chains exist
- worst-case fanout behavior is covered
- docs can point at passing policy tests

tasks.title: Define structured DP and IDP types
tasks.description:
Define structured certificate and CRL distribution-point models. Done when:
- URI shortcuts are replaced by object shapes
- issuingDistributionPoint and reasons masks are modeled
- the clean-break migration is explicit

tasks.title: Parse structured distribution points
tasks.description:
Parse structured distribution points from certificates and CRLs. Done when:
- certificate CRL DPs and CRL IDP structures are preserved
- relative/full names and reasons are available
- fixtures cover varied encodings

tasks.title: Build structured distribution points
tasks.description:
Build distribution-point extensions from the new structured models. Done when:
- certificate and CRL builders emit the new object model
- invalid combinations are rejected
- parse/build round trips stay stable

tasks.title: Enforce CRL applicability rules
tasks.description:
Implement CRL applicability checks before revocation status decisions. Done when:
- issuer, scope, distribution point, reasons, and cRLSign constraints are enforced
- non-applicable CRLs fail as non-applicable
- public results separate applicability from freshness/signature issues

tasks.title: Add indirect CRL support
tasks.description:
Add indirect CRL and certificateIssuer entry processing. Done when:
- indirect CRL entries can identify alternate certificate issuers
- issuer selection logic is tested
- unsupported cases fail clearly

tasks.title: Implement delta CRL merge
tasks.description:
Implement delta CRL processing and merge semantics. Done when:
- complete plus delta combination follows RFC rules
- removeFromCRL is handled correctly
- stale or mismatched delta CRLs are rejected

tasks.title: Expose revocation orchestration API
tasks.description:
Expose high-level revocation orchestration in `src/revocation.ts`. Done when:
- OCSP and CRL evidence compose into good/revoked/unknown style outcomes
- API stays separate from path validation
- docs/types describe evidence vs indeterminate states

tasks.title: Add CRL fixtures
tasks.description:
Add CRL fixtures and end-to-end revocation tests. Done when:
- complete, delta, indirect, and scope-mismatch cases exist
- revocation outcomes are asserted
- tests protect the clean-break DP model

tasks.title: Parse OCSP ResponderID
tasks.description:
Parse OCSP ResponderID by-name and by-key-hash. Done when:
- parsed responses expose responder ID data
- existing response parsing still works
- fixtures cover both forms

tasks.title: Bind signer certificate to ResponderID
tasks.description:
Bind the OCSP signer certificate to ResponderID during validation. Done when:
- same-subject different-key responders fail
- by-name and by-key-hash checks are enforced
- failures are typed

tasks.title: Enforce delegated OCSP responder rules
tasks.description:
Tighten delegated responder authorization rules. Done when:
- direct issuance and ocspSigning EKU are required unless local policy overrides
- issuer-signed responders still pass
- invalid delegated responders fail deterministically

tasks.title: Validate OCSP producedAt
tasks.description:
Validate OCSP producedAt freshness semantics. Done when:
- producedAt is checked alongside thisUpdate and nextUpdate
- clock-skew handling is explicit
- stale and future responses fail predictably

tasks.title: Require full OCSP request coverage
tasks.description:
Require complete request/response CertID coverage. Done when:
- omitted requested CertIDs fail validation
- extra unexpected responses still fail
- multi-cert request fixtures exist

tasks.title: Normalize OCSP validation inputs
tasks.description:
Normalize OCSP validation inputs across DER, PEM, and parsed values. Done when:
- supported input forms share the same validation path
- pre-parsed inputs do not bypass security checks or throw unexpectedly
- tests cover each accepted input shape

tasks.title: Add pure OCSP responder helpers
tasks.description:
Add pure helper APIs for explicit responder selection and discovery inputs. Done when:
- callers can derive responder candidates from certificate metadata without network I/O
- local responder configuration has a typed home
- helpers stay separate from pure validation

tasks.title: Add OCSP fixtures
tasks.description:
Add OCSP auth, completeness, and freshness fixtures. Done when:
- responderID, delegated auth, producedAt, and request-coverage cases exist
- negative cases are explicit
- docs can point at passing OCSP coverage

tasks.title: Parse SRV-ID SAN otherName
tasks.description:
Parse SRV-ID from SAN otherName and add the needed OIDs and types. Done when:
- SRV-ID data is preserved in parsed certificates
- unsupported otherName values are handled intentionally
- fixtures cover SRV-ID encodings

tasks.title: Move DNS and IP matchers
tasks.description:
Move existing DNS/IP identity matching into the identity module. Done when:
- DNS/IP matching no longer lives in raw path validation
- wildcard and IP normalization behavior is preserved or tightened intentionally
- tests move with the new API

tasks.title: Implement URI-ID and SRV-ID matching
tasks.description:
Add URI-ID and SRV-ID service identity matching. Done when:
- URI host/service matching is implemented
- SRV-ID matching follows RFC 6125 rules
- typed failures distinguish identifier-type mismatches

tasks.title: Add IDNA normalization
tasks.description:
Add IDNA normalization for service identity matching. Done when:
- A-label and U-label handling is explicit
- internationalized identifiers compare correctly
- tests cover mixed-case and IDN scenarios

tasks.title: Tighten CN fallback rules
tasks.description:
Make CN fallback a strict compatibility mode. Done when:
- fallback is off by default
- fallback is suppressed when supported SAN identifiers exist
- typed failures explain why fallback was or was not used

tasks.title: Add identity fixtures
tasks.description:
Add RFC 6125 identity fixtures and regressions. Done when:
- DNS, IP, URI, SRV, wildcard, IDNA, and CN-compat cases exist
- positive and negative paths are covered
- docs can claim only shipped identifier types

tasks.title: Document runtime matrix and imports
tasks.description:
Document supported runtimes and import guidance. Done when:
- README includes Node, Bun, Deno, browser, and worker support notes
- WebCrypto/base64/TextEncoder requirements are explicit
- root vs subpath import guidance is documented

tasks.title: Add malformed DER corpus
tasks.description:
Add malformed DER and fuzz-style regression corpus. Done when:
- parsers reject targeted malformed inputs deterministically
- corpus covers certificates, CRLs, OCSP, and policy extensions
- failures do not depend on unsafe exceptions

tasks.title: Add NIST PKITS harness
tasks.description:
Add a NIST PKITS harness for the path-validation claims this repo makes. Done when:
- harness can run relevant PKITS cases
- expected pass/fail mapping is documented
- policy and name-constraint coverage are included once implemented

tasks.title: Add differential test harness
tasks.description:
Add differential tests against at least one mature implementation. Done when:
- chosen oracle implementations are scripted
- core path, CRL, OCSP, and identity outputs can be compared
- mismatches are triaged or documented

tasks.title: Finalize standards claims and matrix
tasks.description:
Finalize public standards claims once conformance gates pass. Done when:
- README, PKIX scope, and future docs match shipped behavior exactly
- compliance matrix is driven by real test status
- no claim remains ahead of implementation
